name: USDJPY regime gate: suppress signals in bull+elevated VIX
result: Removing 50/120 trades (bull+VIX>15) raises USDJPY Sharpe 1.004→1.770. Portfolio 1.2864→1.4396 (+0.153). Largest single improvement since v005.
action: DEPLOYED as v010 in forex_backtester.py USDJPY_VIX_GATE=15.0
mechanism: In bull markets with VIX>15, JPY safe-haven bid fights USD macro signal. Regime mismatch. Filter the cluster, keep the pair.
methodology_note: Original test used batch/fast backtester (no costs, wrong annualization, no OOS holdout). Re-run via: PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/run_hypothesis.py --id HYP-027 --name 'USDJPY regime gate: suppress signals in bull+elevated VIX'

retest_triage.reason: Regime gate (bull AND elevated VIX) is not a single VIX threshold. Its VIX-threshold proxy overlaps HYP-044, already REJECTED_OOS (p=0.50, delta~0). No independent costed re-run available in the forex canonical runner.

name: US10Y divergence from EUR/USD as size modifier (not standalone)
result: Raw IC: 0.086 (below 0.15 gate). Conditional divergence signal (10yr rises >20bps, EUR flat): 70% hit rate n=125, avg -0.921% 15d forward. p<0.0001. Not promotable as standalone — test as size_mult boost layered on existing macro signal.
action: QUEUED — backtest as EUR/USD size_mult=1.25x when divergence event AND macro already aligned
mechanism: US10Y leads EUR/USD by 5-15 days. When they diverge (rate moved, spot hasnt reacted), reversion is high-conviction.
methodology_note: Original test used batch/fast backtester (no costs, wrong annualization, no OOS holdout). Re-run via: PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/run_hypothesis.py --id HYP-028 --name 'US10Y divergence from EUR/USD as size modifier (not standalone)'

retest_triage.reason: US10Y-divergence SIZE MODIFIER. The canonical runner has no size-overlay lever (only VIX/HOLD/TRAILING/EXCLUDE). Would need a sizing-overlay backtester to test.

name: market_structure score is an anti-edge — mirrors HYP-024
result: IC=-0.146 overall. London ms=0: WR=50%, avgR=+1.286 (n=28). London ms>0: WR=22%, avgR=+0.069 (n=73). London UTC03 Grade A ms=0: WR=100%, avgR=+2.875 (n=6). London UTC03 Grade A ms>0: WR=25%, avgR=-0.031 (n=8). A+ London ms>0: WR=17%, avgR=-0.179 (n=36). ROOT CAUSE: BOS/CHOCH confirmation (ms score) = lagging indicator. Trade enters AFTER displacement is done. Same mechanism as pd_alignment (HYP-024).
proposed_action: Set market_structure weight=0.0 in config/ict_params.yml. Same one-line change as HYP-024.
mechanism: ICT scoring rewards confirmation signals that are structurally late. ms=0 trades enter during displacement before structure confirms — that is where the edge lives. ms>0 trades confirm structure has shifted, meaning the move is already committed.
deploy_note: market_structure weight → 0.0. sweep→4.0, fvg_tap→3.0, displacement→1.0. Wired in config/ict_params.yml + ict/pipeline.py 2026-05-23
pvalue_note: market_structure weight — same: parent ICT edge insignificant; moot
methodology_note: Original test used batch/fast backtester (no costs, wrong annualization, no OOS holdout). Re-run via: PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/run_hypothesis.py --id HYP-034 --name 'market_structure score is an anti-edge — mirrors HYP-024'

retest_triage.reason: ICT market_structure feature. Forex canonical runner cannot test ICT features; needs an ICT/structure-aware runner (see futures runner work).

name: XGBoost vs Neural Net R-multiple prediction
hypothesis: H0: XGBoost and 3-layer ReLU perform equally on R-multiple prediction
conclusion: H0 NOT rejected. NN wins by only 0.006 correlation (<<0.05 threshold). XGBoost wins on Sharpe. NN wins on calibration. Keep XGBoost — simpler, better Sharpe, trivial correlation difference.
action: KEEP_XGBOOST — no change to production model

name: Three-confirmation gate (term structure + options skew) for commodity-linked pairs
hypothesis: For AUDUSD and USDCAD: require WTI/copper term structure confirmation + options skew fear zone before entry. All three must align: rate differential signal + term structure + skew.

gates.USDCAD: WTI slope_20 vs slope_60 (curve shape) + OVX in [15,40] fear zone
gates.AUDUSD: Copper slope_20 vs slope_60 + VIX in [12,30] fear zone

AUDUSD.verdict: HURT — copper gate redundant with existing VIX regime gate

USDCAD.verdict: CONFIRMED — term structure removes ~71% of signals, WR lifts from 41.8% to 51.2%

conclusion: Term structure gate is a genuine edge for USDCAD: Sharpe +0.375, WR +9.4pp. AUDUSD already has VIX regime gate — copper filter is redundant and hurts. Apply USDCAD gate only.
action: Wire WTI term structure gate for USDCAD only. Test USDCAD re-entry into portfolio at gated Sharpe 0.701. Do NOT apply copper gate to AUDUSD.
next_hypothesis: HYP-031: USDCAD portfolio re-entry — does adding USDCAD at 0.701 Sharpe improve portfolio Sharpe from 1.72 via diversification benefit?
pvalue_note: Three-confirmation term-structure gate — bespoke, not generically toggleable
methodology_note: Original test used batch/fast backtester (no costs, wrong annualization, no OOS holdout). Re-run via: PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/run_hypothesis.py --id HYP-030 --name 'Three-confirmation gate (term structure + options skew) for commodity-linked pairs'

retest_triage.reason: UNRECONSTRUCTABLE: bespoke 3-confirmation gate (term structure + options skew). No data feed or config lever exists for it.

name: USDCAD portfolio re-entry with term structure gate
hypothesis: Adding USDCAD at gated Sharpe 0.701 to 5-pair v012 portfolio improves portfolio Sharpe.
conclusion: Diversification win: USDCAD hurts the simple avg Sharpe metric (-0.169) but improves true portfolio Sharpe (+0.015) via decorrelation. CAD/oil is uncorrelated enough with the EUR/GBP/JPY rate pairs that it adds genuine diversification. The improvement is small (0.015) — not enough to justify complexity yet.
action: HOLD: do not add USDCAD in v013. The diversification win (+0.015 true Sharpe) is real but marginal. Revisit if USDCAD gated Sharpe improves above 1.0 with additional signal refinement.
note: Key insight: true portfolio Sharpe (2.76) is significantly higher than avg individual Sharpe (1.72) — confirming the pairs are meaningfully decorrelated. This is already institutional-grade diversification.
methodology_note: Original test used batch/fast backtester (no costs, wrong annualization, no OOS holdout). Re-run via: PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/run_hypothesis.py --id HYP-031 --name 'USDCAD portfolio re-entry with term structure gate'

retest_triage.reason: USDCAD is not in ALL_PAIRS universe AND term-structure gate has no config lever. Not expressible without a universe change + a new gate type.

name: Stochastic Drift Decomposition — SNR entry gate
hypothesis: H0: Separating drift from Brownian noise (SNR = |μ|/σ) produces no improvement in directional prediction accuracy vs baseline. H1: When SNR > 1.5, directional accuracy improves by > 10pp.
method: Fit SDE dX=μdt+σdW. Estimate μ=20d rolling mean, σ=20d rolling std, shifted by 1 day (no lookahead). SNR=|μ|/σ daily. Split trades: HIGH_SNR>1.5 vs LOW_SNR≤1.5. Compare win rate, avg R, Sharpe. Two-sample t-test on R-multiples. Holdout: 2024 only. Script: scripts/hyp_032_snr_gate.py
accept_if: p<0.05 AND delta_R>0.15 AND delta_WR>10pp AND holdout replicates
practical_implication: Add SNR>1.5 entry gate. Only fire trades in high drift-to-noise regime.
connection: Layer 1 of 3-stack with HYP-033 and HYP-036. Tells WHEN to trade.
conclusion: H0 NOT REJECTED. SNR p75 threshold=0.2531. Train: delta_R=-0.155, delta_WR=-0.7pp, p=0.1469. HIGH_SNR regime (top quartile of |μ|/σ) slightly underperforms LOW_SNR on daily forex data. Drift signal on 20-day rolling window is insufficient to constrain direction on daily returns — noise dominates at this timescale. The SDE decomposition is valid theory but wrong application layer for daily holds.
action: CLOSE. Do not add SNR gate. Consider testing on weekly returns or longer hold periods where drift accumulates relative to noise.

name: Monte Carlo State Interference — path convergence size modulator
hypothesis: H0: MC path convergence score at entry has no relationship to subsequent trade outcome. H1: When >65% of 1000 simulated forward paths agree on direction within hold period, WR exceeds baseline by >8pp.
method: At each entry, simulate 1000 GBM paths using prior-window μ and σ (lagged 1d, no lookahead). convergence_score = max(long_fraction, short_fraction). HIGH_CONV: score>0.65. Spearman rho between convergence and R-multiple. Bin analysis: [0.50-0.55][0.55-0.60][0.60-0.65][0.65-0.70][0.70-0.80][0.80+]. Holdout: 2024. Script: scripts/hyp_033_mc_convergence.py
accept_if: Spearman rho>0.15 AND p<0.05 AND delta_WR>8pp at HIGH_CONV AND holdout replicates
connection: Layer 2 of 3-stack with HYP-032 and HYP-036. Tells HOW MUCH CONFIDENCE to have.
conclusion: H0 NOT REJECTED. GBM path convergence (N=1000) shows no positive relationship to trade outcome. Train: rho=-0.018 p=0.0608. Holdout: rho=-0.026 p=0.0045. Convergence score is structurally capped near 0.65-0.70 (GBM with estimated daily μ/σ rarely produces >70% path agreement). The 0.65 HIGH_CONV threshold selected almost no trades (-48.9pp vs baseline). The concept depends on HYP-032 being true — if SNR doesn't predict direction, convergence score inherits that failure.
action: CLOSE. Path convergence from pure GBM on daily returns has no edge. Would require regime-conditional μ to be meaningful.
pvalue_note: Null result (no latent feature) — nothing to test

name: Random Matrix Theory Noise Filtering — RMT covariance cleaning
hypothesis: H0: RMT-cleaned covariance matrix produces no improvement in risk-adjusted returns vs raw covariance. H1: RMT eigenvalue cleaning reduces max drawdown by >15% while maintaining Sharpe.
method: Build 252-day rolling correlation matrix for 5 pairs. Apply Marchenko-Pastur filter (q=T/N=252/5=50.4, λ_max=(1+√(1/q))²≈1.302). Eigendecompose, clean noise eigenvalues below λ_max, reconstruct. Run parallel backtests: Portfolio A uses raw C for sizing, Portfolio B uses C_clean. Same signals and entries, different sizing only. Paired t-test on monthly returns. Holdout: 2024.
accept_if: Drawdown reduction > 15% AND Sharpe maintained AND correlation matrix more stable in stress
practical_implication: Replace covariance input in kelly_engine.py with RMT-cleaned matrix recomputed monthly.
connection: Layer 3 of 3-stack. Independent of HYP-032/033 — can deploy standalone. Tells HOW TO SIZE correctly.
conclusion: H0 NOT REJECTED for performance metrics. RMT cleaning produces no material Sharpe or drawdown improvement via per-trade correlation penalty sizing. However: (1) 30.2% of correlation matrix variance is confirmed as MP noise, (2) COVID stress test shows +13.7% correlation matrix stability gain — real signal for risk management during drawdowns. The hypothesis was testing the wrong application layer. RMT benefit is NOT in per-trade size adjustments (too granular) — it is in portfolio-level Kelly fraction optimization and drawdown management.
action: REFRAME: Do not use RMT for per-trade sizing. Use for monthly Kelly fraction optimization — replace portfolio covariance input in risk engine with RMT-cleaned matrix. Build HYP-036: test RMT at portfolio allocation level (monthly rebalance) vs drawdown-period risk reduction.
key_finding: Signal eigenvalue structure is real: 2 of 5 eigenvalues survive MP filter. 30% of apparent correlation is noise. The application layer was wrong in this test, not the theory.
pvalue_note: RMT covariance cleaning — bespoke, not generically toggleable
methodology_note: Original test used batch/fast backtester (no costs, wrong annualization, no OOS holdout). Re-run via: PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/run_hypothesis.py --id HYP-035 --name 'Random Matrix Theory Noise Filtering — RMT covariance cleaning'

retest_triage.reason: RMT covariance cleaning is a portfolio-construction method, not a per-pair config lever. Not expressible in the canonical runner.

name: RMT Covariance at Portfolio Kelly Level — monthly rebalance
hypothesis: H0: RMT-cleaned covariance at monthly portfolio Kelly rebalance produces no improvement vs raw covariance. H1: RMT cleaning reduces drawdown >15% during stress periods while maintaining Sharpe.
method: HYP-035 tested per-trade correlation penalty — wrong application layer. This tests portfolio-level: monthly rebalance of Kelly fractions using RMT-cleaned C vs raw C. Track cumulative drawdown during stress windows (COVID, 2022 rate shock). Script: scripts/hyp_036_rmt_portfolio_kelly.py
accept_if: Drawdown materially lower during stress AND Sharpe maintained AND 2024 holdout replicates
practical_implication: Replace portfolio covariance input in kelly_engine.py with RMT-cleaned matrix recomputed monthly.
connection: Layer 3 of 3-stack. Reframe of HYP-035 (PARTIAL_CONFIRMED) at correct application layer. Tells HOW TO SIZE correctly.
result: H1 REJECTED: holdout Sharpe delta -0.103, no stress drawdown improvement >15%

name: fvg_tap anti-edge is timing-conditional
statement: The overall IC=-0.056 for fvg_tap is driven by bad timing windows (UTC 04xx, NY session). Within UTC 03xx (live gate), fvg_tap=2 performs well: WR=50% avgR=+1.208 (n=18). The component should NOT be zeroed out — it's only harmful when combined with bad timing.
test_method: IC analysis + conditional breakdown on 208 trades (Window A+B)
result: fvg_tap=2 in UTC 03xx: WR=50% avgR=+1.208 (n=18). Overall IC=-0.056.
conclusion: No action needed. Current system already constrains timing — fvg_tap anti-edge is already neutralized by timing gate.
impact: Validates existing architecture. Prevents premature zeroing of fvg_tap weight.
pvalue_note: fvg_tap timing — same: parent ICT edge insignificant; moot
methodology_note: Original test used batch/fast backtester (no costs, wrong annualization, no OOS holdout). Re-run via: PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/run_hypothesis.py --id HYP-037 --name 'fvg_tap anti-edge is timing-conditional'

retest_triage.reason: ICT fvg_tap timing feature. Forex canonical runner cannot test ICT features; needs an ICT-aware runner.

name: displacement=0 as standalone veto
statement: Trades with displacement component score = 0 systematically underperform: WR=27.8% avgR=-0.076 (n=36) vs displacement>0 WR=33.7% avgR=+0.294 (n=172). A veto on displacement=0 regardless of overall grade may improve EV without reducing trade count excessively.
test_method: Pending — needs impact analysis on London A dataset specifically
result: Global: delta=+0.038 (below threshold). London A+A+ only: delta=+0.059. Not wiring as universal veto.
impact: TBD — could remove ~17% of trades. If Grade A displacement=0 n is small, impact is minimal.

name: UTC 15xx as second valid London close window
statement: UTC 15xx Grade A: WR=55% avgR=+0.300 (n=20). This is the London close / early NY overlap window. If this holds in walk-forward, it may be a second valid entry window distinct from the vetoed NY_PM session.
test_method: Pending — needs walk-forward validation across both A and B windows
result: UTC 15xx avgR=+0.090 — not a viable second window. UTC 03xx gate confirmed as sole valid window.
impact: Could add ~10-15% more valid trade opportunities if confirmed

name: Macro Signal Freshness Gate
h1: Fresh macro signals (1-2d) produce higher R-multiples than stale signals (5+d)
result: ARCHITECTURALLY VOID — all 648 trades tagged as 1-2d fresh. Macro signals fire monthly at BMS; fast backtester enters on bar i+1. Every entry is maximally fresh by design. No staleness variation exists to exploit.
verdict: REJECTED — architecture void. Oracle estimated day-1/day-4 WR decay but v013 has no deferred entries. Freshness gate not applicable to monthly-resample signal architecture.

name: VIX Gate Threshold Sweep — USDJPY/AUDNZD refinement
h1: Lowering USDJPY/AUDNZD VIX gate threshold from 15→13 improves Sharpe by removing confused-regime trades
result: CONFIRMED — USDJPY VIX>13: 46 trades, 67.4% WR, Sharpe 2.979 (was 1.770 at VIX>15). AUDNZD VIX>13: 39 trades, 53.8% WR, Sharpe 2.246 (was 1.558). Portfolio 1.8552→2.097 (+0.242). v014 gate cleared.
verdict: CONFIRMED — implemented as v014. USDJPY and AUDNZD thresholds 15→13. Monotonic improvement across all tested thresholds (8-15).
revision_2026_05_31: ⚠️ DID NOT VALIDATE OUT-OF-SAMPLE. On the 2023-2024 holdout the VIX 15→13 gate shows delta=0.000 (v014 vs v013 identical). The '+0.242' was in-sample noise. Costed √n-weighted portfolio Sharpe is 0.72 IS / 1.07 OOS, not 2.097. Candidate for retirement — see holdout_validation_v014.json.

name: Displacement Gate >= 1.5 improves London trade quality
h1: Excluding London trades with displacement < 1.5 improves avgR and WR
result: Baseline London n=172 WR=31% avgR=+0.448 → Gate kept n=139 WR=35% avgR=+0.594. Removed 33 trades: WR=15% avgR=-0.167. Net +0.146R per kept trade, 81% pass rate.
verdict: CONFIRMED — gate is clear and non-destructive. Wire disp>=1.5 soft veto in pipeline Stage 3 after Colin review.
impact: Expected +0.146R/trade on London universe. Requires human confirmation before live wire.
pvalue_note: displacement gate — same: parent ICT edge insignificant; moot

name: ICT Score Inversion — high score anti-edge within high-displacement trades
h1: Within high-displacement (disp>=1.5) London trades, ICT score is inversely correlated with performance
verdict: CONFIRMED — score anti-edge is monotonic and statistically robust. Ultra-high-score setups may be over-obvious (professionals already positioned). The A+ downgrade decision was correct but insufficient.
impact: URGENT review needed. Potential score cap at score<9.0 for entry decisions. Do NOT wire without Colin's deliberate review — this changes fundamental grading logic.
pvalue_note: ICT score inversion — same: parent ICT edge insignificant; moot

name: Short natural-duration trades underperform — entries before commitment formed
hypothesis: GBPUSD trades that close naturally within 3 days consistently underperform trades held 4+ days. Fast closures = stops or TP1 scalps that never developed momentum, suggesting entry before full commitment formed.
result: Surface finding from L-5 retro validation 2026-05-27. Rule tested: pair-specific hold<=N underperforms forensics baseline (delta_sharpe=-0.087, p=0.008). Backtester contradicts the rule-as-written (forced exits improve Sharpe), but the forensics signal — natural short-duration trades underperform — may be real and separate from the hold cap effect. n=~310 ICT trades. Needs clean isolation: split forensics by natural_hold_days, compare R-multiple distribution 1-3d vs 4+d, control for session and grade.
action: Run forensics split: group ICT trades by hold_days bucket (1-3, 4-7, 8+). Compare avg R-multiple and Sharpe within London+GradeA subset. If 4+ days consistently outperforms: the commitment detector may need a minimum-bars-since-signal gate at entry. Minimum sample per bucket: 20 trades.
mechanism: Fast natural closures are stops or TP1 scalps — price moved briefly in the right direction then reversed. These trades entered before institutional commitment was fully expressed. The commitment score was passing but the actual momentum had not formed yet. A minimum hold expectation gate (bars_since_signal or displacement threshold) at entry might filter these.
notes: REST MODE 2026-06-02: Permutation test confirmed (p<0.0001, N=10000). Short-hold trades (1-3d): WR=14.3%, avg=-0.42%, t=-4.19. Long-hold (4d+): WR=74.1%, avg=+0.47%, t=+3.68. Monotonic across all 5 pairs. Primary short-exit reason: reversal (59%), meaning trade went wrong-way immediately. Pre-entry gate needed: recommend minimum conviction threshold study. Action: Add hold_days_expected gate — veto CB-signal trades where macro momentum score < 0.50 (low commitment proxy).

name: Tuesday+Thursday DOW veto — London ICT
result: CONFIRMED. Tue WR=11.5%/-0.490R, Thu WR=14.3%/-0.214R. Walk-forward replicated in Window A and B independently. DOW veto alone: MC=98.6%, 3.2/mo, G2 in 9mo. Dominates UTC03 gate at 3x frequency.
pvalue_note: DOW veto — same: parent ICT edge insignificant; moot

name: Day-of-month bias: days 1-7 negative, days 8-15 exceptional
result: CANDIDATE. Days 1-7: WR=15.0%, avgR=-0.175 (n=20). Days 8-15: WR=51.5%, avgR=+1.538 (n=33). n=20 insufficient for gate. Monitor 3 more months.
notes: REST MODE 2026-06-02: Rejected as confounded. DOM distribution: 362/421 trades (86%) cluster on days 3-5 (CB decision window). Days 8-31 have n=35 total — too sparse to test. The perceived day-of-month effect is CB calendar structure, not a separate DOM edge. The candidate is absorbed into SEED-CAL-001/002 which are already embedded in the system.

name: AUDNZD exclusion (4-pair)
retest_note: Canonical re-run 2026-06-02 returned oos_delta=0.0 ONLY because the live universe is already 4-pair (AUDNZD excluded) — excluding an already-excluded pair is a no-op, not a reversal. OOS Sharpe 1.0811, permutation p=0.0033 REAL, BH survives, decay ROBUST. Stamped via save_version. Edge stands.

name: EUR/GBP hold 5d — without AUDNZD
EUR/GBP hold 7d — without AUDNZD
EUR/GBP hold 10d — without AUDNZD
Rate differential trend gate (30d momentum)
note: 30-day cross-pair trend gate: 1/4 OOS years aligned. Rejected. See HYP-052b for reformulation.

name: Rate differential volatility gate (30d rolling std)
note: REST-003 analysis on 502 trades (4-pair, 2015-2024): HIGH rate-vol trades have LOWER or equal Sharpe vs LOW-vol at every threshold. delta ranges from -0.04 to -0.40. Opposite of REST-002 prediction. Rate vol correlates with regime but does not predict individual trade outcomes. See FIND-REST-003-b.

name: Pair-level rate trend gate (>50% days widening per pair)
note: Pair-level version of HYP-052: only trade pair X when majority of recent days show widening rate diff for that pair. Correctly calls 2024 (all 4 pairs block), correctly identifies USDJPY as 2022 winner, correctly selects EURUSD+USDJPY for 2023. Needs run_with_signals() to backtest formally.

result.note: OOS > IS — no overfit. Enter only when |real_rate_diff| widening vs previous trade for that pair.

name: Rate level gate: require |real_rate_diff| > 1.0% before macro swing entry
hypothesis: Requiring |real_rate_diff| > 1.0% at entry as a hard gate substantially improves selectivity. Trades where |rd| < 1%% have near-random Sharpe (0.199). Trades where |rd| > 1%% have Sharpe 0.650. Delta = +0.451. 65%% of trades pass (326/502 over 10yr). Economic rationale: when rate differential is small, carry is minimal and macro signal has insufficient edge over currency volatility risk.
next_step: Implement in signal_engine.py: after computing real_rate_diff, gate out signal if |real_rate_diff| < 1.0%. Run OOS holdout (2023-2024) to validate. If confirmed, bump to CONFIRMED and add to ForexBacktester as RATE_LEVEL_GATE.
note: CONFIRMED via REST-004 OOS validation (rq_rest_009). 4-pair portfolio (502 trades 2015-2024): gated Sharpe 0.207 vs baseline 0.154 full period. OOS (2023-2024): gated 0.267 vs blocked -0.117. 2024 specifically: 0.029→0.192. IS delta (+0.039) < OOS delta (+0.084) — no overfitting. Ready for v016 implementation in signal_engine.py.
implementation: In signal_engine.py, after computing real_rate_diff: if abs(real_rate_diff) < 1.0: return None  # insufficient carry edge

name: Rate differential acceleration (WIDENING > NARROWING)
methodology_note: Net expectancy over ALL trades (winners+losers); 10k-shuffle permutation on mean-R diff; v015 4-pair OOS n=103; features reconstructed. Pre-registered: WIDENING (real_rate_diff 10-calendar-day delta > 0) has higher mean R

name: Volatility regime (MED outperforms LOW & HIGH tails)
methodology_note: Net expectancy over ALL trades (winners+losers); 10k-shuffle permutation on mean-R diff; v015 4-pair OOS n=103; features reconstructed. Pre-registered: MED_VOL (0.4% ≤ ATR_14d < 0.7%) mean R exceeds LOW and HIGH

name: Currency momentum as an orthogonal overlay on carry (WITH vs AGAINST 63d spot momentum)
methodology_note: Net expectancy over ALL trades; 10k-shuffle permutation; v015 4-pair OOS n=103; fully costed; features reconstructed. Grounded in Menkhoff, Sarno, Schmeling & Schrimpf (2012), 'Currency Momentum Strategies'. Time-series momentum-AGREEMENT overlay on single carry trades, NOT the cross-sectional winner-minus-loser factor (4 pairs can't form that). Prior anchor: COUNTER_MOMENTUM group did better here (0.747 vs 0.242 R).

name: Overnight effect on QQQ (orthogonal non-FX source)
result: overnight 5.494bp/day (cum 3169%, Sharpe 0.971) vs intraday 0.088bp; net-1.5x Sharpe 0.574; perm p=0.004899510048995101; recent_alive=True
methodology_note: QQQ daily adj OHLC, overnight=open/prior-close, intraday=close/open; fully costed (1x/1.5x/2x); per-year walk-forward; segment-label permutation. Grounded in Cooper/Cliff/Gulen; Lou, Polk & Skouras 'A Tug of War' (2019); Branch & Ma — overnight/intraday return decomposition. CAVEAT: phenomenon on Nasdaq source, not yet a tradeable NQ strategy (needs futures feed/broker; 24h structure may weaken).

name: Does overnight-QQQ diversify the carry edge (crisis-conditional)?
decision: REJECTED as a carry diversifier (kill accepted). overnight-QQQ remains a VALID_EDGE standalone, but is NOT a crisis-independent second edge: long-only is correlated return-stacking (rho~0.36, worse in crisis); long-short looks orthogonal in calm (rho 0.014) but robustly re-couples in the COVID liquidity crash (rho 0.416, BH p=0.0072, sig>calm). Caveat: measured vs DBV proxy (died 2023-03); real v015 overlap thin (n=39, rho=0.036).
result: long-only full rho=0.361 maxCrisis=0.568; long-short full rho=0.014 maxCrisis=0.416. NEITHER version diversifies carry in crisis — overnight-QQQ is correlated return-stacking, not true diversification. Do NOT treat it as a crisis-independent second edge.
methodology_note: QQQ overnight & overnight−intraday vs DBV carry proxy; full + crisis (2008/2020/2022) + VIX>30 + tail-decile + rolling-60d correlations. Lou, Polk & Skouras 'A Tug of War' (2019); overnight/intraday decomposition + carry-factor (DBV) diversification. DBV≠our exact strategy; crisis corr decides.

robustness.method: moving-block bootstrap CIs + crisis-vs-calm diff test + BH (scripts/diversification_robustness.py)
robustness.finding: Market-neutral long-short COVID coupling rho=0.416 is STATISTICALLY ROBUST (CI [0.112,0.631] excludes 0; BH p=0.0072; sig > calm). NOT a 50-day fluke. It coupled in the COVID liquidity crash (was independent/negative in 2008 & 2022), but coupling in the sharpest crash is disqualifying for a crisis-independent diversifier. Prior kill CONFIRMED & hardened. overnight-QQQ = return-stacking, one edge, not two. Caveat unchanged: DBV proxy; live carry book has no crisis history.

title: USDJPY + USDCAD macro contrarian filter: only trade MVD=-1
description: For USDJPY and USDCAD, only enter when macro_vs_direction=-1 (macro signal points against the trade — contrarian entry). USDJPY MVD=-1: IS=1.324, OOS=2.101. USDJPY MVD=1: IS=-1.356, OOS=0.680. USDCAD MVD=-1: IS=0.768, OOS=1.299. USDCAD MVD=1: IS=-0.157, OOS=0.044. Needs formal permutation test. GBPUSD and EURUSD do not show the same stable pattern.
test_needed: rq_rest_010_macro_contrarian_gate.py — permutation test on MVD=-1 filter for USDJPY+USDCAD
note: PARTIAL_CONFIRMATION via REST-006. USDJPY MVD=-1 vs +1: delta=+0.406, p=0.0117 (significant). USDCAD: delta=+0.184, p=0.175 (not significant). Combined USDJPY+USDCAD full-period: p=0.012. OOS test underpowered (n=15 MVD=-1 OOS trades, p=0.21). 2024 HURTS with this filter: MVD=-1 Sharpe=-0.115 vs baseline 0.091. Adding to 3-gate stack: OOS unchanged (0.315→0.316). DO NOT implement as hard gate. Consider USDJPY-only size modifier. USDCAD needs 2026+ live data for confirmation.

title: Counter-momentum entry: trade against 63d price trend for better timing
description: Counter-momentum entries (where price has NOT moved in the macro signal direction over 63d) outperform momentum-aligned entries. Full period: counter Sharpe=0.163 vs aligned=0.088 (p=0.185). OOS: counter=0.343 vs aligned=0.044 (p=0.039 — significant). Per-pair: EURUSD p=0.037 (significant), USDCAD p=0.128, USDJPY REVERSED (aligned better, p=0.877). Combined with 2-gate stack (widening + rate level): OOS 0.315→0.499 with momentum-counter filter. IS=0.272 < OOS=0.499: no overfit. 2024: 0.001→0.233 (doesn't hurt problem year). Permutation within 2-gate universe: p=0.065 (marginal). OOS permutation: p=0.101 (underpowered, n=21).
test_needed: Additional 2025-2026 live trades needed for statistical confirmation. Near-term: add momentum_63d logging to decision_logger for EURUSD trades so live validation begins.

title: Sovereign Core (master-ml-archive) MoE — does it have edge?
description: Costed permutation kill-gate on the archived Sovereign Core MoE (Hurst router + XGBoost specialists). Signal-mode (models isolated from the sizing throttle = most generous test), clean frozen yfinance daily bars, 7 liquid ETFs (SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA/XLK/XLF/XLE), in-sample 2015-2022, 1000 permutations, 30bp round-trip cost.
note: NO_EDGE (p=0.164) — signal indistinguishable from random entries and Sharpe-negative after costs. Router tautology confirmed (OOS 0.998 — labels derive from the hurst_short input feature; csd/hmm/adx hardcoded constants). Cold-start EV gate (Kelly/Hoeffding lower-bound, n_trades=20) blocks trade #1, so the README's '200 paper trades to go live' is unreachable. DO NOT revive. Evidence: data/research/sovereign_core_verdict.md; harness: data/research/sovereign_core_gauntlet/.

title: Per-pair hold overrides (v007, GBP6/AUD5/EUR5) vs 60d baseline — redesigned validation
description: The 'pending redesigned validation' the code comment calls for. Backtester is the only valid test for exit rules (retro_validate's forensics test was self-acknowledged invalid). TREATMENT (live overrides) vs BASELINE (all 60d), 4-pair, costed, OOS 2023-24 + 10k random-cap permutation + per-year walk-forward.
note: NOT_SIGNIFICANT — fails walk-forward stability. Benefit is regime-concentrated (2021 +0.145, 2023 +0.123; 2022 -0.045, 2024 0.000) and GBPUSD-driven (+0.34) partly offset by AUDUSD (negative). Overrides near-inert on realized hold duration (only AUDUSD avg shifts 5.9->4.6); GBPUSD gain comes from capping non-event signals, not from a clean duration limit. CAVEAT: harness OOS treatment=1.305/n=110 does NOT reconcile to the 2026-06-02 holdout 1.081/n=103 (likely yfinance data drift); the treatment-vs-baseline DELTA is the valid internal measure. Permutation p=0.044 (chosen vs random caps) marginally passed but the question that matters (robust vs 60d) fails walk-forward. Recommend roll back to 60d pending confirmation.
